strengths: resolveMessageAction is an exported pure function with all four documented outcomes (send/confirm/cancel/invalid), correctly trims the message and treats whitespace-only/unknown-action/missing bodies as invalid (routes.ts:204-215)
Backward compatibility with { message: string } is preserved — string message branch maps to { kind: 'send', message: trimmed } (routes.ts:209-212)

All three DB dep helpers exist with the exact documented SQL shapes: selectConversationHistory (routes.ts:221-241); deleteOutputsForBoard (routes.ts:243-250); deleteCardsForBoard via column_id IN (SELECT id FROM columns WHERE board_id=$1) (routes.ts:252-261)
All three helpers wired into realDeps via pool, plus classifyFollowUpIntent imported from ./llm.js (routes.ts:284,469-473)
Message endpoint is thin glue over resolveMessageAction: confirm/cancel/send/invalid->400, and forwards { status: 404 } via the existing 'status' in result check (routes.ts:550-580)
Route is reused (no new endpoint added); no business logic in routes — all delegated to service
Tests written following the established exported-pure-helper + fakeDb convention; all 16 routes.test.ts tests pass
